content:
VESPRO™ LIVEZYMES
Fresh Fruits & Vege Enzyme Powder
A potent broad-spectrum plant-based enzyme blend important for the digestion and absorption of all major nutrients.

VesPro LiveZymes is specially formulated for the Asian diet. It contains 14 different types of digestive enzymes from a 14 day fermentation process of: Papaya, Pineapple, Avocado, Almond, Barley, Banana, Cucumber, Maize, Purple Grapes, Peas, Soy Bean & Malt.

Potency of VesPro LiveZymes:

Each serving contains 14 types of high potency (100% FCC Food Chemical Codex Standard) digestive enzymes. Enzymes that don't list FCC units or only list weight (like milligrams) don't tell you about the enzyme's activity and strength.

Enzyme Types and Potency:
- Acid Maltase: 28 MaltU (digests maltose)
- Lipase: 7000 FIP (digests fats)
- Protease A: 42,000 HUT (digests proteins)
- Protease B: 8000 PC (digests proteins)
- Protease C: 100 SAPU (digests proteins)
- Peptidase: 4 AP (digests peptides into amino acids)
- Alpha Amylase: 10,000 SKB (digests starch and oligosaccharides)
- Glucoamylase: 18 AGU (digests complex carbohydrates)
- Alpha Galactosidase: 300 GaU (digests beans and legumes)
- Lactase: 2000 ALU (digests lactose)
- Invertase: 800 SU (digests sucrose)
- Cellulase: 6000 CU (digests insoluble fiber)
- Hemicellulase: 2000 HCU (digests high-fiber foods)
- Pectinase: 15000 ADJU (digests plant cell walls)

Benefits:

- Reduction in indigestion & heartburn problems
- Relief from gas, bloating, constipation & diarrhea
- High in proteolytic enzymes for increased heart health
- Diminished food allergies (proteins are more completely digested)
- Increased energy levels (nutrients are better absorbed)
- Support healthy weight loss
- Relieve over burdened pancreas
- Fast and immediate detoxification when taken on empty stomach
- Reduce inflammation with high proteolytic enzymes

Key Enzymes Explained:

Lipase - Digests Fats: Lipase is the digestive enzyme that digests fats. All proteins and starches contain fat. Because meat is not used in cooking, vegetarian food in restaurants contains particularly high amounts of oil to enhance flavor. However, the oil in food reduces the activity of all types of digestive enzymes. Therefore, lipase is needed to break down fats or triglycerides into simple fatty acids.

Proteases - Digests Proteins/Anti-inflammatory: Proteases are the digestive enzymes that digest proteins. Protein is the most complex macro-nutrient and different stages of protein digestion in the stomach and small intestine are handled by different types of proteolytic enzymes. Using a single type of proteolytic enzyme to digest complex protein is insufficient as it can only handle a specific stage of protein digestion.

VesPro LiveZymes includes 3 types of proteases operating in different pH ranges:
1. Acid Protease (pH 2-6) - works in the stomach
2. Neutral Protease (pH 6.5-8) - works in the intestines
3. Alkaline Protease (pH 8-11) - works in the small intestine

Alpha Galactosidase - Digests Beans and Legumes: Alpha galactosidase is an expensive enzyme used to digest beans and legumes of all types. It is very important for the Asian vegetarian market. It digests soybeans, peas, cabbage and broccoli thereby preventing gas and flatulence problems. Alpha galactosidase is rarely used in most European digestive enzyme formulas. VesPro LiveZymes is specially formulated for the Asian diet.

Cellulase - Digests Insoluble Fiber: Cellulase is the digestive enzyme that digests insoluble fiber. It converts insoluble cellulose from vegetables, fruit peels and cereals into simple glucose. Cellulase is very important and essential for vegetarians. European or American enzyme formulas use less cellulase because their diet emphasizes less vegetables.

The Role of Enzymes in Healthy Weight Loss:

Lipase and protease are essential enzymes for supporting healthy weight loss. Unprocessed food is rich in lipase. However, most of the food we eat is processed, so we lack enough lipase to burn the fat in our normal diet. When our diet is rich in fat and lipase is insufficient, our body cannot burn excess fat and convert it to energy. Instead, fat accumulates and is stored in arteries, organs and capillaries – visible as build-up on hips, buttocks, stomach and thighs.

Proteases are crucial enzymes that break down proteins and eliminate toxins. When excess toxins are stored in fat, burning fat becomes very difficult. Therefore, proteases or protease supplements help eliminate toxins embedded in fat.

Another angle to weight loss is food digestion and absorption. When your body cannot absorb enough nutrients from food, it receives a signal that it needs to store more fat to prepare for hunger threats, and will insist on needing more fat. It will also signal needing more food. This can lead to a vicious cycle of eating more and more food but still feeling hungry often. Correct digestive enzyme supplementation can stop such unhealthy response, help restore normal body function and keep you healthier.

How Do You Know If You Are Enzyme Deficient?

Due to age, our body gradually loses its ability to produce enzymes. At first it may not be obvious, but slowly you will notice you can't enjoy certain foods like before. There is also a feeling of declining energy.

Early signs of enzyme deficiency are digestive problems such as heartburn, gas, constipation, bloating, diarrhea and abdominal pain. Other symptoms may include headaches, allergies, ulcers, yeast infections, chronic fatigue, nutrient deficiency and lack of energy.
